sourceQuestion:
A CPU has a cache with block size 64 bytes. The main memory has 8 banks, each bank being 8 bytes wide. Consecutive 8-byte chunks are mapped on consecutive banks with wrap-around. All the banks can be accessed in parallel, but two accesses to the same bank must be serialized. A cache block access may involve multiple iterations of parallel bank accesses. The number of iterations required to fetch a cache block is:

A. 4
B. 8
C. 16
D. cannot be determined

parsed.stem: A CPU has a cache with block size 64 bytes. The main memory has 8 banks, each bank being 8 bytes wide. Consecutive 8-byte chunks are mapped on consecutive banks with wrap-around. All the banks can be accessed in parallel, but two accesses to the same bank must be serialized. A cache block access may involve multiple iterations of parallel bank accesses. The number of iterations required to fetch a cache block is:

sourceQuestion:
The chip select logic for a certain DRAM chip in a memory system design is shown below. Assume that the memory system has 16 address lines denoted by A_15 to A_0. The chip select (CS) logic uses a NAND gate whose inputs are A_15, NOT(A_14), A_13, NOT(A_12) and an AND gate with A_11, NOT(A_10). The CS is active low. What is the range of addresses (in hexadecimal) of the memory system that can get enabled by the chip select (CS) signal?

A. C800 to CFFF
B. CA00 to CAFF
C. C800 to C8FF
D. DA00 to DFFF

parsed.stem: The chip select logic for a certain DRAM chip in a memory system design is shown below. Assume that the memory system has 16 address lines denoted by A_15 to A_0. The chip select (CS) logic uses a NAND gate whose inputs are A_15, NOT(A_14), A_13, NOT(A_12) and an AND gate with A_11, NOT(A_10). The CS is active low. What is the range of addresses (in hexadecimal) of the memory system that can get enabled by the chip select (CS) signal?

sourceQuestion:
A 4 kilobyte byte-addressable memory is realized using four 1K x 8 memory blocks. Two input address lines are connected to the chip select port of these memory blocks through a 2-to-4 decoder as shown in the figure. The remaining ten input address lines from IA11-IA0 (where IA11 and IA10 go to decoder, IA9-IA0 go to address port of each block). The chip select (CS) is active high.

The input memory address (IA11-IA0), in decimal, for the starting locations (Addr = 0) of each block (indicated as X1, X2, X3, X4) among the options given below is CORRECT?

A. (0,1,2,3)
B. (0,1024,2048,3072)
C. (0,5,1024,2048)
D. (0,0,0,0)

parsed.stem:
A 4 kilobyte byte-addressable memory is realized using four 1K x 8 memory blocks. Two input address lines are connected to the chip select port of these memory blocks through a 2-to-4 decoder as shown in the figure. The remaining ten input address lines from IA11-IA0 (where IA11 and IA10 go to decoder, IA9-IA0 go to address port of each block). The chip select (CS) is active high.

The input memory address (IA11-IA0), in decimal, for the starting locations (Addr = 0) of each block (indicated as X1, X2, X3, X4) among the options given below is CORRECT?

sourceQuestion:
A microprogrammed control unit
A. Is faster than a hard-wired control unit.
B. Facilitates easy implementation of new instruction.
C. Is useful when very small programs are to be run.
D. Usually refers to the control unit of a microprocessor.
sourceAnswer: B. Facilitates easy implementation of new instruction.

parsed.stem: A microprogrammed control unit

options.text: Is faster than a hard-wired control unit.
Facilitates easy implementation of new instruction.
Is useful when very small programs are to be run.
Usually refers to the control unit of a microprocessor.

sourceQuestion:
A micro program control unit is required to generate a total of 25 control signals. Assume that during any micro instruction, at most two control signals are active. Minimum number of bits required in the control word to generate the required control signals will be:
A. 2
B. 2.5
C. 10
D. 12

parsed.stem: A micro program control unit is required to generate a total of 25 control signals. Assume that during any micro instruction, at most two control signals are active. Minimum number of bits required in the control word to generate the required control signals will be:

sourceQuestion:
A micro instruction is to be designed to specify:
a. none or one of the three micro operations of one kind
b. none or up to six micro operations of another kind
The minimum number of bits in the micro-instruction is:
A. 9
B. 5
C. 6
D. None of the above

parsed.stem:
A micro instruction is to be designed to specify:
a. none or one of the three micro operations of one kind
b. none or up to six micro operations of another kind
The minimum number of bits in the micro-instruction is:

sourceQuestion:
Arrange the following configuration for CPU in decreasing order of operating speeds:
Hard wired control, Vertical microprogramming, Horizontal microprogramming.
A. Hard wired control, Vertical microprogramming, Horizontal microprogramming.
B. Hard wired control, Horizontal microprogramming, Vertical microprogramming.
C. Horizontal microprogramming, Vertical microprogramming, Hard wired control.
D. Vertical microprogramming, Hard wired control, Horizontal microprogramming.
sourceAnswer: B. Hard wired control, Horizontal microprogramming, Vertical microprogramming.

parsed.stem:
Arrange the following configuration for CPU in decreasing order of operating speeds:
Hard wired control, Vertical microprogramming, Horizontal microprogramming.

options.text: Hard wired control, Vertical microprogramming, Horizontal microprogramming.
Hard wired control, Horizontal microprogramming, Vertical microprogramming.
Horizontal microprogramming, Vertical microprogramming, Hard wired control.
Vertical microprogramming, Hard wired control, Horizontal microprogramming.

sourceQuestion:
Horizontal microprogramming:
A. does not require use of signal decoders
B. results in larger sized microinstructions than vertical microprogramming
C. uses one bit for each control signal
D. all of the above

parsed.stem: Horizontal microprogramming:

options.text: does not require use of signal decoders
results in larger sized microinstructions than vertical microprogramming
uses one bit for each control signal

sourceQuestion:
The microinstructions stored in the control memory of a processor have a width of 36 bits. Each microinstruction is divided into three fields: a micro-operation field of 23 bits, a next address field (X), and a MUX select field (Y). There are 8 status bits in the input of the MUX. How many bits are there in X and Y, and what is the size of the control memory in number of words?
A. 10, 3, 1024
B. 8, 5, 256
C. 5, 8, 2048
D. 10, 3, 512

parsed.stem: The microinstructions stored in the control memory of a processor have a width of 36 bits. Each microinstruction is divided into three fields: a micro-operation field of 23 bits, a next address field (X), and a MUX select field (Y). There are 8 status bits in the input of the MUX. How many bits are there in X and Y, and what is the size of the control memory in number of words?

sourceQuestion:
Consider the following sequence of micro-operations.
MBR <- PC
MAR <- X
PC <- Y
Memory <- MBR

Which one of the following is a possible operation performed by this sequence?
A. Instruction fetch
B. Operand fetch
C. Conditional branch
D. Initiation of interrupt service

parsed.stem:
Consider the following sequence of micro-operations.
MBR <- PC
MAR <- X
PC <- Y
Memory <- MBR

Which one of the following is a possible operation performed by this sequence?

sourceQuestion:
A CPU has only three instructions I1, I2 and I3, which use the following signals in time steps T1-T5:
I1: T1: Ain, Bout, Cin  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: (T1+T2).T3 = T1,T1  T4: Bin, Cout  T5: End
I2: T1: Aout, Bin  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: Din, Aout  T4: Bin, Cout  T5: End
I3: T1: Din, Aout  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: Din, Aout  T4: Zin, Aout  T5: End

Which of the following pairs of expressions represent the circuit for generating control signals S_30 and S_40 respectively?
A. S_30 = T3 + I1*T3 and S_40 = T1*(I1+I2)*T4
B. S_30 = (I1+I2)*T3 and S_40 = T1*(I1+I2)*T4
C. S_30 = T1 + I2*T3 and S_40 = T1*(I1+I2)*T4
D. S_30 = T1 + (I2+I3)*T3 and S_40 = (I1+I2)*T4

parsed.stem:
A CPU has only three instructions I1, I2 and I3, which use the following signals in time steps T1-T5:
I1: T1: Ain, Bout, Cin  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: (T1+T2).T3 = T1,T1  T4: Bin, Cout  T5: End
I2: T1: Aout, Bin  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: Din, Aout  T4: Bin, Cout  T5: End
I3: T1: Din, Aout  T2: PCout, Bin  T3: Din, Aout  T4: Zin, Aout  T5: End

Which of the following pairs of expressions represent the circuit for generating control signals S_30 and S_40 respectively?

sourceQuestion:
A hardwired CPU uses 10 control signals S_1 to S_10 in various time steps T_1 to T_5 to implement 4 instructions I_1 to I_4 as shown below:

         T_1          T_2          T_3          T_4          T_5
I_1:  S_1,S_2,S_3   S_4         S_5          S_6,S_8      S_7
I_2:  S_1,S_2       S_3,S4,S_5  S_8          S_9          S_10
I_3:  S_1,S_5,S_3   S_2         S_5          S_6,S_9      S_10
I_4:  S_1,S_5,S_3   S_4         S_5          S_8          S_7

(I_f, T_f) indicates that the control signal should be generated in time step T_f if the instruction being executed is I_f.

Which of the following pairs of expressions represent the circuit for generating control signals S_1 and S_9 respectively?

A. S_1 = T_1 + I_2.T_2 + T_3  and  S_9 = (I_2 + I_3).T_4
B. S_1 = (I_1 + I_4).T_1 + (I_1 + I_2).T_2  and  S_9 = (I_2 + I_3).T_4
C. S_1 = T_1 + (I_2 + I_4).T_1  and  S_9 = (I_2 + I_3).T_4
D. S_1 = (I_1 + I_2 + I_3 + I_4).T_1  and  S_9 = (I_2 + I_3).T_4

parsed.stem:
A hardwired CPU uses 10 control signals S_1 to S_10 in various time steps T_1 to T_5 to implement 4 instructions I_1 to I_4 as shown below:

         T_1          T_2          T_3          T_4          T_5
I_1:  S_1,S_2,S_3   S_4         S_5          S_6,S_8      S_7
I_2:  S_1,S_2       S_3,S4,S_5  S_8          S_9          S_10
I_3:  S_1,S_5,S_3   S_2         S_5          S_6,S_9      S_10
I_4:  S_1,S_5,S_3   S_4         S_5          S_8          S_7

(I_f, T_f) indicates that the control signal should be generated in time step T_f if the instruction being executed is I_f.

Which of the following pairs of expressions represent the circuit for generating control signals S_1 and S_9 respectively?

sourceQuestion:
An instruction set of a processor has 125 signals which can be divided into 5 groups of mutually exclusive signals as follows:
Group 1: 20 signals
Group 2: 70 signals
Group 3: 2 signals
Group 4: 10 signals
Group 5: 23 signals

How many bits of the control words can be saved by using vertical microprogramming over horizontal microprogramming?

A. 0
B. 22
C. 102
D. 125

parsed.stem:
An instruction set of a processor has 125 signals which can be divided into 5 groups of mutually exclusive signals as follows:
Group 1: 20 signals
Group 2: 70 signals
Group 3: 2 signals
Group 4: 10 signals
Group 5: 23 signals

How many bits of the control words can be saved by using vertical microprogramming over horizontal microprogramming?

sourceQuestion:
The data path shown in the figure computes the number of 1s in the 32-bit input word corresponding to an unsigned even integer stored in the shift register. The unsigned counter, initially zero, is incremented if the most significant bit of the shift register is 1. The microprogram for the control is shown in the table below:

| Microinstruction | Reset_Counter | Shift_left | Load_output |
|-----------------|---------------|------------|-------------|
| BEGIN           | 1             | 0          | 0           |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| ...             | ...           | ...        | ...         |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| END             | 0             | 0          | 1           |

The counter width (k), the number of missing microinstructions (n), and the control word for microinstructions I1, I2, ..., In are, respectively.

A. 32,5,010
B. 5,32,010
C. 5,31,011
D. 5,31,010

parsed.stem:
The data path shown in the figure computes the number of 1s in the 32-bit input word corresponding to an unsigned even integer stored in the shift register. The unsigned counter, initially zero, is incremented if the most significant bit of the shift register is 1. The microprogram for the control is shown in the table below:

| Microinstruction | Reset_Counter | Shift_left | Load_output |
|-----------------|---------------|------------|-------------|
| BEGIN           | 1             | 0          | 0           |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| ...             | ...           | ...        | ...         |
| I1              | 0             | 1          | 0           |
| END             | 0             | 0          | 1           |

The counter width (k), the number of missing microinstructions (n), and the control word for microinstructions I1, I2, ..., In are, respectively.

sourceQuestion:
Consider a CPU where all the instructions require 7 clock cycles to complete execution. There are 120 instructions in the instruction set. It is found that 140 control signals are needed to be generated by the control unit. While designing the horizontal microprogrammed control unit, single address field format is used for conditional branching. What is the size (in bits) of the control memory and the size (in bits) of the address field of the microinstruction, respectively?

A. 125, 7
B. 125, 10
C. 135, 9
D. 135, 10

parsed.stem: Consider a CPU where all the instructions require 7 clock cycles to complete execution. There are 120 instructions in the instruction set. It is found that 140 control signals are needed to be generated by the control unit. While designing the horizontal microprogrammed control unit, single address field format is used for conditional branching. What is the size (in bits) of the control memory and the size (in bits) of the address field of the microinstruction, respectively?

sourceQuestion:
An instruction pipeline consists of 4 stages: Fetch (F), Decode field (D), Execute (E) and Result Write (W). The instructions in a certain instruction sequence need these stages for the following number of clock cycles:

| Instruction | F | D | E | W |
|-------------|---|---|---|---|
| 1           | 1 | 2 | 1 | 1 |
| 2           | 1 | 1 | 3 | 1 |
| 3           | 2 | 1 | 2 | 1 |
| 4           | 1 | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| 5           | 1 | 2 | 1 | 2 |

Find the number of clock cycles needed to complete the 5 instructions.

A. 13
B. 14
C. 15
D. 16

parsed.stem:
An instruction pipeline consists of 4 stages: Fetch (F), Decode field (D), Execute (E) and Result Write (W). The instructions in a certain instruction sequence need these stages for the following number of clock cycles:

| Instruction | F | D | E | W |
|-------------|---|---|---|---|
| 1           | 1 | 2 | 1 | 1 |
| 2           | 1 | 1 | 3 | 1 |
| 3           | 2 | 1 | 2 | 1 |
| 4           | 1 | 3 | 2 | 1 |
| 5           | 1 | 2 | 1 | 2 |

Find the number of clock cycles needed to complete the 5 instructions.

sourceQuestion:
Comparing the time T1 taken for a single instruction on a pipelined CPU with time T2 taken on a non-pipelined but identical CPU, we can say that

A. T1 <= T2
B. T1 >= T2
C. T1 < T2
D. T1 = T2 plus the time taken for one instruction fetch cycle

parsed.stem: Comparing the time T1 taken for a single instruction on a pipelined CPU with time T2 taken on a non-pipelined but identical CPU, we can say that

options.text: T1 = T2 plus the time taken for one instruction fetch cycle

sourceQuestion:
The performance of a pipelined processor suffers if:
A. the pipeline stages have different delays
B. consecutive instructions are dependent on each other
C. the pipeline stages share hardware resources
D. All of the above

parsed.stem: The performance of a pipelined processor suffers if:

options.text: the pipeline stages have different delays
consecutive instructions are dependent on each other
the pipeline stages share hardware resources

sourceQuestion:
For a pipelined CPU with a single ALU, consider the following situations:
I. The j-th instruction is the 1st instruction and uses the result of the (j-1)-th instruction as an operand
II. The execution of a conditional jump instruction
III. The j-th and (j+1)-th instructions require the ALU at the same time
Which of the above can cause a hazard?
A. I and II only
B. II and III only
C. III only
D. All of I, II and III
sourceAnswer: D. All of I, II and III

parsed.stem:
For a pipelined CPU with a single ALU, consider the following situations:
I. The j-th instruction is the 1st instruction and uses the result of the (j-1)-th instruction as an operand
II. The execution of a conditional jump instruction
III. The j-th and (j+1)-th instructions require the ALU at the same time
Which of the above can cause a hazard?

options.text: All of I, II and III

sourceQuestion:
A 4-stage pipeline has the stage delays as 100, 160, 140 and 100 nanoseconds respectively. Registers that are used between the stages have a delay of 5 nanoseconds each. Assuming constant clocking rate, the total time taken to process 1000 data items on this pipeline will be:
A. 120.4 microseconds
B. 165.0 microseconds
C. 160.5 microseconds
D. 590.0 microseconds

parsed.stem: A 4-stage pipeline has the stage delays as 100, 160, 140 and 100 nanoseconds respectively. Registers that are used between the stages have a delay of 5 nanoseconds each. Assuming constant clocking rate, the total time taken to process 1000 data items on this pipeline will be:

sourceQuestion:
A 5-stage pipelined CPU has the following sequence of stages:
IF - Instruction fetch from instruction memory
RD - Instruction decode and register read
EX - Execute: ALU operation for data and address computation
MA - Data memory access; for write access, the register read at RD stage is used
WB - Register write back

Consider the following sequence of instructions:
I1: L R0, loc1    (R0 <- M[loc1])
I2: A R0, R0      (R0 <- R0 + R0)
I3: S R2, R0      (R2 <- R2 - R0)

Let each clock cycle take 1 ns. What is the number of clock cycles taken to complete the above sequence of instructions starting from the fetch of I1?
A. 8
B. 19
C. 12
D. 15

parsed.stem:
A 5-stage pipelined CPU has the following sequence of stages:
IF - Instruction fetch from instruction memory
RD - Instruction decode and register read
EX - Execute: ALU operation for data and address computation
MA - Data memory access; for write access, the register read at RD stage is used
WB - Register write back

Consider the following sequence of instructions:
I1: L R0, loc1    (R0 <- M[loc1])
I2: A R0, R0      (R0 <- R0 + R0)
I3: S R2, R0      (R2 <- R2 - R0)

Let each clock cycle take 1 ns. What is the number of clock cycles taken to complete the above sequence of instructions starting from the fetch of I1?

sourceQuestion:
A CPU has a five-stage pipeline and runs at 1 GHz frequency. Instruction fetch happens in the first stage of the pipeline. A conditional branch instruction computes the target address and evaluates the condition in the third stage of the pipeline. The processor stops fetching new instructions following a conditional branch until the branch outcome is known. A program executes 10^9 instructions out of which 20% are conditional branches. If each instruction takes one clock cycle and a conditional branch causes a 2-cycle stall, what is the total execution time of the program?
A. 1.0 seconds
B. 1.2 seconds
C. 1.4 seconds
D. 1.6 seconds

parsed.stem: A CPU has a five-stage pipeline and runs at 1 GHz frequency. Instruction fetch happens in the first stage of the pipeline. A conditional branch instruction computes the target address and evaluates the condition in the third stage of the pipeline. The processor stops fetching new instructions following a conditional branch until the branch outcome is known. A program executes 10^9 instructions out of which 20% are conditional branches. If each instruction takes one clock cycle and a conditional branch causes a 2-cycle stall, what is the total execution time of the program?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a pipelined processor with the following four stages:
IF: Instruction Fetch
ID: Instruction Decode and Operand Fetch
EX: Execute
WB: Write Back
The IF, ID and WB stages take one clock cycle each to complete the operation. The number of clock cycles for the EX stage depends on the instruction. The ADD and SUB instructions need 1 clock cycle and the MUL instruction needs 3 clock cycles in the EX stage. Operand forwarding is used in the pipelined processor. What is the number of clock cycles taken to complete the following sequence of instructions?

ADD  R2, R1, R0    R2 <- R1+R0
MUL  R4, R3, R2    R4 <- R3*R2
SUB  R6, R5, R4    R6 <- R5-R4

A. 7
B. 8
C. 10
D. 14

parsed.stem:
Consider a pipelined processor with the following four stages:
IF: Instruction Fetch
ID: Instruction Decode and Operand Fetch
EX: Execute
WB: Write Back
The IF, ID and WB stages take one clock cycle each to complete the operation. The number of clock cycles for the EX stage depends on the instruction. The ADD and SUB instructions need 1 clock cycle and the MUL instruction needs 3 clock cycles in the EX stage. Operand forwarding is used in the pipelined processor. What is the number of clock cycles taken to complete the following sequence of instructions?

ADD  R2, R1, R0    R2 <- R1+R0
MUL  R4, R3, R2    R4 <- R3*R2
SUB  R6, R5, R4    R6 <- R5-R4

sourceQuestion:
Which of the following are NOT true in a pipelined processor?
I. Bypassing can handle all RAW hazards
II. Register renaming can eliminate all register carried WAR hazards
III. Control hazard penalties can be eliminated by dynamic branch prediction

A. I and II only
B. I and III only
C. II and III only
D. I, II and III

parsed.stem:
Which of the following are NOT true in a pipelined processor?
I. Bypassing can handle all RAW hazards
II. Register renaming can eliminate all register carried WAR hazards
III. Control hazard penalties can be eliminated by dynamic branch prediction

sourceQuestion:
Delayed branching can help in the handling of control hazards. For all delayed conditional branch instructions, irrespective of whether the condition evaluates to true or false:
A. The instruction following the conditional branch instruction in memory is executed
B. The first instruction in the fall through path is executed
C. The first instruction in the taken path is executed
D. The branch takes longer to execute than any other instruction
sourceAnswer: A. The instruction following the conditional branch instruction in memory is executed

parsed.stem: Delayed branching can help in the handling of control hazards. For all delayed conditional branch instructions, irrespective of whether the condition evaluates to true or false:

options.text: The instruction following the conditional branch instruction in memory is executed
The first instruction in the fall through path is executed
The first instruction in the taken path is executed
The branch takes longer to execute than any other instruction

sourceQuestion:
Delayed branching can help in the handling of control hazards. The following code is to run on a pipelined processor with one branch delay slot:

I1: ADD R1 <- R7 + R8
I2: SUB R4 <- R5 - R8
I3: ADD R6 <- R2 + R3
I4: STORE Memory[R4] <- R5
    BRANCH to Label if R1 == 0

Which of the instructions I1, I2, I3 or I4 can legitimately occupy the delay slot without any program modification?

A. I1
B. I2
C. I3
D. I4

parsed.stem:
Delayed branching can help in the handling of control hazards. The following code is to run on a pipelined processor with one branch delay slot:

I1: ADD R1 <- R7 + R8
I2: SUB R4 <- R5 - R8
I3: ADD R6 <- R2 + R3
I4: STORE Memory[R4] <- R5
    BRANCH to Label if R1 == 0

Which of the instructions I1, I2, I3 or I4 can legitimately occupy the delay slot without any program modification?

sourceQuestion:
Consider a 4-stage pipeline processor. The number of cycles needed by the four instructions I1, I2, I3, I4 in stages S1, S2, S3, S4 is shown below:

        S1  S2  S3  S4
  I1:    1   2   1   1
  I2:    1   3   2   2
  I3:    2   1   1   3
  I4:    1   2   2   2

What is the number of cycles needed to execute the following loop?
For (i = 1 to 2) { I1; I2; I3; I4; }

A. 16
B. 23
C. 28
D. 30

parsed.stem:
Consider a 4-stage pipeline processor. The number of cycles needed by the four instructions I1, I2, I3, I4 in stages S1, S2, S3, S4 is shown below:

        S1  S2  S3  S4
  I1:    1   2   1   1
  I2:    1   3   2   2
  I3:    2   1   1   3
  I4:    1   2   2   2

What is the number of cycles needed to execute the following loop?
For (i = 1 to 2) { I1; I2; I3; I4; }

sourceQuestion:
A 5-stage pipelined processor has stages: Instruction Fetch (IF), Instruction Decode (ID), Operand Fetch (OF), Perform Operation (PO), and Write Operand (WO). The IF, ID, OF and WO stages take 1 clock cycle each for any instruction. The PO stage takes 1 clock cycle for ADD and SUB instructions, 3 clock cycles for MUL instruction, and 6 clock cycles for DIV instruction. Operand forwarding is used in the pipeline. What is the number of clock cycles needed to execute the following sequence of instructions?

Instruction        Meaning of Instruction
t1: MUL R1,R2,R3   R1 = R2 * R3
t2: DIV R1,R4,R1   R1 = R4 / R1
t3: ADD R5,R6,R1   R5 = R6 + R1
t4: SUB R5,R5,R7   R5 = R5 - R7

A. 13
B. 15
C. 17
D. 19

parsed.stem:
A 5-stage pipelined processor has stages: Instruction Fetch (IF), Instruction Decode (ID), Operand Fetch (OF), Perform Operation (PO), and Write Operand (WO). The IF, ID, OF and WO stages take 1 clock cycle each for any instruction. The PO stage takes 1 clock cycle for ADD and SUB instructions, 3 clock cycles for MUL instruction, and 6 clock cycles for DIV instruction. Operand forwarding is used in the pipeline. What is the number of clock cycles needed to execute the following sequence of instructions?

Instruction        Meaning of Instruction
t1: MUL R1,R2,R3   R1 = R2 * R3
t2: DIV R1,R4,R1   R1 = R4 / R1
t3: ADD R5,R6,R1   R5 = R6 + R1
t4: SUB R5,R5,R7   R5 = R5 - R7

sourceQuestion:
Consider an instruction pipeline with four stages (S1, S2, S3 and S4) each with combinational circuit only. The pipeline registers are required between each stage and at the end of the last stage. Delays for the stages and for the pipeline registers are as given in the figure.

Stage delays: S1 = 5 ns, S2 = 6 ns, S3 = 11 ns, S4 = 8 ns
Pipeline register delay: 1 ns each (5 registers total, one after each stage)

What is the approximate speed up of the pipeline in steady state under ideal conditions when compared to the corresponding non-pipeline implementation?

A. 4.0
B. 2.5
C. 1.1
D. 3.0

parsed.stem:
Consider an instruction pipeline with four stages (S1, S2, S3 and S4) each with combinational circuit only. The pipeline registers are required between each stage and at the end of the last stage. Delays for the stages and for the pipeline registers are as given in the figure.

Stage delays: S1 = 5 ns, S2 = 6 ns, S3 = 11 ns, S4 = 8 ns
Pipeline register delay: 1 ns each (5 registers total, one after each stage)

What is the approximate speed up of the pipeline in steady state under ideal conditions when compared to the corresponding non-pipeline implementation?

sourceQuestion:
Register renaming is done in pipelined processors:

A. as an alternative to register allocation at compile time
B. for efficient access to function parameters and local variables
C. to handle certain kinds of hazards
D. as part of address translation
sourceAnswer: C. to handle certain kinds of hazards

parsed.stem: Register renaming is done in pipelined processors:

options.text: as an alternative to register allocation at compile time
for efficient access to function parameters and local variables
to handle certain kinds of hazards
